question: In Adobe Character Animator, how do you adjust the sensitivity and camera input settings for the Head Turner behavior?
answer: Hover over the 'Head Turner [Default]' option in the 'Properties' panel on the right, adjust the 'Sensitivity' slider to 25%, then click the 'Camera Input' checkbox to enable/disable head tracking.
keypoints: The cursor hovers over the 'Head Turner [Default]' option within the 'Properties' panel on the right side of the screen.
The user adjusts the 'Sensitivity' slider under the 'Head Turner [Default]' settings to a value of 25%, decreasing the head turning sensitivity of the character.
The user clicks on the 'Camera Input' checkbox within the 'Head Turner [Default]' settings, enabling or disabling the camera input for head tracking.

question: In Adobe Character Animator, how do you prepare a puppet and review its animation sequence?
answer: Click the 'Preparing "Starfall"' button at the bottom to initiate puppet preparation, then drag the timeline slider in the 'Timeline Scrub' bar to review the character's animation sequence.
keypoints: The user clicks on the 'Preparing "Starfall"' button at the bottom of the screen, initiating the preparation process for the selected puppet.
The user drags the timeline slider in the 'Timeline Scrub' bar at the bottom of the screen, reviewing the animation sequence of the character.

question: In Adobe Character Animator, how do you record and demonstrate real-time head movement tracking?
answer: Click the 'Record' button at the bottom of the timeline to start recording, then move your head in front of the camera to make the character's head turn in real-time based on your movements.
keypoints: The user clicks on the 'Record' button at the bottom of the timeline, initiating the recording process for the character's movements.
The user moves their head in front of the camera, demonstrating how the character's head turns in real-time based on the recorded movements.

question: In Adobe Character Animator, how do you access layer details after switching to the Rig tab?
answer: Click the 'Rig' tab at the top of the interface to enter the rigging section, then scroll through the layers list to reveal character components like 'Hair', 'Eyes', and 'Mouth'.
keypoints: The user clicks on the 'Rig' tab at the top of the interface, switching the view to the rigging section where detailed layer settings are visible.
The user scrolls through the layers list in the 'Rig' section, revealing various parts of the character like 'Hair', 'Eyes', 'Mouth', etc.
